recommendations.title: Blood Meridian
recommendations.author: Cormac McCarthy
recommendations.reason: Given their interest in dark, literary fiction with historical settings like 'The Vaster Wilds' and 'One Foot in Eden,' McCarthy's brutal and poetic masterpiece about violence on the American frontier would resonate deeply.

recommendations.title: A Gentleman in Moscow
recommendations.author: Amor Towles
recommendations.reason: Their appreciation for richly atmospheric, character-driven historical fiction like 'Imperium,' 'Augustus,' and 'The Poisonwood Bible' points strongly toward this elegant novel of a Russian count under house arrest.

recommendations.title: The Little Stranger
recommendations.author: Sarah Waters
recommendations.reason: Since 'Fingersmith' by Sarah Waters is already on their want-to-read list and they enjoy gothic, atmospheric horror like 'Dark Matter' and 'We Have Always Lived in the Castle,' this haunted-house psychological thriller is an ideal companion recommendation.

recommendations.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy
recommendations.author: John le Carré
recommendations.reason: Since 'Our Kind of Traitor' by John le Carré is on their want-to-read list and 'The Spy Who Came In From The Cold' is already read, this masterful Cold War espionage novel is a natural next step in their exploration of le Carré's work.

recommendations.title: The Terror of Living
recommendations.author: Urban Waite
recommendations.reason: Their taste for tense, literary crime fiction with a strong sense of place—seen in 'Slow Horses,' 'Eye of the Needle,' and 'One Foot in Eden'—makes this spare, atmospheric Pacific Northwest thriller a compelling fit.

recommendations.title: Riddley Walker
recommendations.author: Russell Hoban
recommendations.reason: Their interest in dystopian and post-apocalyptic fiction like 'The Circle,' 'Lord of the Flies,' and '2030,' combined with their appetite for formally experimental literary work like 'The Pale King,' makes this uniquely voiced post-nuclear novel a natural match.

recommendations.title: The Name of the Rose
recommendations.author: Umberto Eco
recommendations.reason: Their love of intellectually rich historical mysteries with gothic atmosphere—evident from 'The Monk,' 'Fingersmith,' and 'The Shadow of the Wind' (read)—makes this medieval monastery murder mystery an ideal recommendation.

recommendations.title: A Wrinkle in Time
recommendations.author: Madeleine L'Engle
recommendations.reason: Their broad interest spanning science fiction, fantasy, and coming-of-age narratives seen across authors like Ursula K. Le Guin adjacent picks, 'The Night Circus,' and 'Rivers of London' suggests this beloved classic blend of science and wonder would appeal strongly.

recommendations.title: The Terror of Living
recommendations.author: Urban Waite
recommendations.reason: Fans of tense, atmospheric thrillers like Eye of the Needle and Slow Horses will find this rural crime noir equally gripping and darkly literary.

recommendations.title: The Watchmaker of Filigree Street
recommendations.author: Natasha Pulley
recommendations.reason: Its blend of Victorian atmosphere, quiet fantasy, and intricate plotting mirrors the appeal of The Night Circus and Fingersmith already on their list.

recommendations.title: The Word Exchange
recommendations.author: Alena Graedon
recommendations.reason: Their interest in dystopian and media-culture themes seen in The Circle and Screen People makes this near-future thriller about language collapse a natural fit.

recommendations.title: Snow Crash
recommendations.author: Neal Stephenson
recommendations.reason: Already drawn to Zodiac and Stephenson's satirical sci-fi voice, they would find this sharp, prophetic cyberpunk novel equally thrilling and darkly comic.

recommendations.title: Lanark: A Life in Four Books
recommendations.author: Alasdair Gray
recommendations.reason: Their appetite for challenging, structurally inventive literary fiction like The Pale King and House of Leaves makes this dark Scottish modernist epic a compelling match.

recommendations.title: The Rules of the Game
recommendations.author: Bret Easton Ellis
recommendations.reason: Their interest in morally complex, darkly literary fiction like The Rules of Attraction and Lolita makes this unflinching, satirical portrait of American decadence a natural fit.

recommendations.title: Riddley Walker
recommendations.author: Russell Hoban
recommendations.reason: Their interest in dystopian literary fiction like The Road and Lord of the Flies points toward this inventive, linguistically dazzling post-apocalyptic classic.

recommendations.title: The Unconsoled
recommendations.author: Kazuo Ishiguro
recommendations.reason: Their appetite for surreal, structurally challenging literary fiction like The Magus and The Pale King makes this dreamlike, disorienting Ishiguro novel an ideal next read.

recommendations.author: Russell Hoban
recommendations.reason: Their interest in dystopian literary fiction like The Road and Lord of the Flies points toward this inventive, linguistically dazzling post-apocalyptic classic.

recommendations.title: The Unconsoled
recommendations.author: Kazuo Ishiguro
recommendations.reason: Their appetite for surreal, structurally challenging literary fiction like The Magus and The Pale King makes this dreamlike, disorienting Ishiguro novel an ideal next read.

recommendations.title: The Passage of Power
recommendations.author: Robert A. Caro
recommendations.reason: Their interest in sweeping historical narratives like Undaunted Courage, Augustus, and Fall of Giants suggests they would be captivated by this masterful account of LBJ's rise and the Kennedy assassination.

recommendations.title: The Raw Shark Texts
recommendations.author: Steven Hall
recommendations.reason: Their love of mind-bending metafictional works like House of Leaves, Piranesi, and How to Live Safely in a Science Fictional Universe points directly to this inventive thriller about a man hunted by a conceptual shark.

recommendations.title: The Helliconia Trilogy
recommendations.author: Brian W. Aldiss
recommendations.reason: Their want-to-read list includes Aldiss's Hothouse alongside epic science fantasy like Hyperion and Leviathan Wakes, making this sweeping civilization-spanning saga a natural fit.

recommendations.title: The Historian
recommendations.author: Elizabeth Kostova
recommendations.reason: Their taste for atmospheric literary horror with historical depth, as seen with The Terror, The Shadow of the Wind already read, and Fingersmith on their list, aligns perfectly with this richly researched gothic thriller.

recommendations.title: Riddley Walker
recommendations.author: Russell Hoban
recommendations.reason: Their dystopian science fiction appetite spanning Parable of the Sower, The Road already read, and The Deluge on their list, combined with their literary fiction sensibility, makes this post-apocalyptic masterpiece of invented language essential.

recommendations.title: The Children of Men
recommendations.author: P.D. James
recommendations.reason: Their sustained interest in dystopian fiction from Brave New World to The Handmaid's Tale adjacent works, paired with their fondness for British literary thrillers like Slow Horses and Our Kind of Traitor, makes this near-future extinction novel a compelling fit.

recommendations.title: Pillars of the Earth
recommendations.author: Ken Follett
recommendations.reason: Having Fall of Giants and Eye of the Needle on their want-to-read list by the same author, and loving epic historical fiction and adventure, this sweeping medieval saga of cathedral-building and political intrigue is an essential next step.

recommendations.title: Suttree
recommendations.author: Cormac McCarthy
recommendations.reason: Having read Blood Meridian, The Road, and All the Pretty Horses, and with their clear appetite for literary fiction from Absalom Absalom to The Pale King, this richly lyrical McCarthy novel about an outcast living on the margins of society is a natural recommendation.

recommendations.author: Robert A. Caro
recommendations.reason: Their interest in sweeping historical narratives like Undaunted Courage, Augustus, and Fall of Giants suggests they would be captivated by this masterful account of LBJ's rise and the Kennedy assassination.

recommendations.author: P.D. James
recommendations.reason: Their sustained interest in dystopian fiction from Brave New World to The Handmaid's Tale adjacent works, paired with their fondness for British literary thrillers like Slow Horses and Our Kind of Traitor, makes this near-future extinction novel a compelling fit.

recommendations.title: Pillars of the Earth
recommendations.author: Ken Follett
recommendations.reason: Having Fall of Giants and Eye of the Needle on their want-to-read list by the same author, and loving epic historical fiction and adventure, this sweeping medieval saga of cathedral-building and political intrigue is an essential next step.

recommendations.title: Suttree
recommendations.author: Cormac McCarthy
recommendations.reason: Having read Blood Meridian, The Road, and All the Pretty Horses, and with their clear appetite for literary fiction from Absalom Absalom to The Pale King, this richly lyrical McCarthy novel about an outcast living on the margins of society is a natural recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Terror of Living
recommendations.author: Urban Waite
recommendations.reason: Shares the taut, bleak crime atmosphere of S.A. Cosby and Dennis Lehane that appears repeatedly on their list, set against a harsh wilderness backdrop similar to Peter Heller's work they've enjoyed.

recommendations.title: The Raw Shark Texts
recommendations.author: Steven Hall
recommendations.reason: A metafictional thriller that sits at the intersection of House of Leaves, Cloud Atlas, and the David Mitchell novels they consistently seek out, with a conceptual horror element that matches their taste.

recommendations.title: The Book of the New Sun
recommendations.author: Gene Wolfe
recommendations.reason: A dense, literary science fantasy that would appeal to their love of Hyperion, The Way of Kings, and Wolfe's reputation for layered, unreliable narration mirrors their interest in challenging literary fiction.

recommendations.title: Sabriel
recommendations.author: Garth Nix
recommendations.reason: A dark, atmospheric fantasy with a unique magic system involving death that mirrors their interest in The Night Circus, The Magicians, and other imaginative adventure-driven fantasy on their list.

recommendations.title: Mexican Gothic
recommendations.author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
recommendations.reason: Given their enjoyment of Silver Nitrate by the same author and their strong pattern of interest in atmospheric gothic horror like Dark Matter, The September House, and Our Wives Under the Sea, this would be a natural fit.

recommendations.title: Perdido Street Station
recommendations.author: China Miéville
recommendations.reason: This sprawling, dark, and inventive weird fiction novel connects directly to their interest in Imajica, Leviathan Wakes, and the more transgressive, literary end of fantasy and science fiction on their list.

recommendations.title: The Handmaid's Tale
recommendations.author: Margaret Atwood
recommendations.reason: Their repeated interest in dystopian literary fiction like Parable of the Talents, Chain-Gang All-Stars, and The Deluge, combined with having read Oryx and Crake, makes this a clear next Atwood to seek out.

recommendations.title: We Need to Talk About Kevin
recommendations.author: Lionel Shriver
recommendations.reason: The dark psychological depth, unreliable narration, and literary crime elements connect to their interest in books like My Dark Vanessa, These Violent Delights, and the true crime nonfiction like Columbine on their list.
